actions_taken: Raised min_confidence from 0.55 to 0.60 in /home/ubuntu/trading/ai-trading-bot/src/agents/risk_config.yaml. Rationale: max_position_pct (the 12:30 lever) bounds HOW LARGE a new entry can be, but does not bound WHETHER a marginal-conviction entry qualifies at all — and the post-12:30 pattern shows the strategy layer filling right up to the new ceiling rather than pulling back. min_confidence is the complementary gate: it raises the bar on qualification. Non-punitive: already-accepted OPEN orders (NESTLEIND, AUBANK x2) are unaffected, and Day-1 history shows today's conviction distribution clears 0.60 for legitimate signal.

Retained max_position_pct = 0.15 set at 12:30. Working as a hard ceiling (AUBANK pending 13.21% is the tightest test and is compliant); no reason to tighten further this tick — the new lever should act first.
Did NOT tighten max_deployed_pct (0.80): projected deployment on full fill is 48.88%, still well inside ceiling with ~₹2.5L+ headroom. Tightening now would be preemptive.
Did NOT tighten daily_loss_limit_pct (0.03) or drawdown thresholds (10% / 15%): realized P&L is ₹0 and no drawdown has materialized. Tightening loss gates with zero losses is noise.

Did NOT tighten default_sl_pct (0.02) or min_risk_reward (1.5): no evidence that exits or R:R quality are the risk vector — the vector is concentration/correlation, and min_confidence addresses it at the qualification layer. Avoid piling restrictions on uncorrelated axes.
